prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for formaldehyde (H₂C=O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 for the neutral (cation: charge +1 mult 2; anion: charge −1 mult 2). Charges were partitioned by the Mulliken scheme. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file with no re-optimization (single-point Fukui). All three SCF calculations converged. Integrity status: trustworthy: true; all checks passed (fukui_charge_conservation_f+ sum=1.0000, fukui_charge_conservation_f- sum=1.0000, schema_header present). No warnings were reported by the engine.

Key results (Mulliken-partitioned Fukui indices):
- O (atom 1): f⁺ = 0.46814, f⁻ = 0.46742, f⁰ = 0.46778, Δf = +0.00071
- C (atom 2): f⁺ = 0.70839, f⁻ = 0.11244, f⁰ = 0.41042, Δf = +0.59595
- H (atom 3): f⁺ = −0.08827, f⁻ = 0.21008, f⁰ = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29835
- H (atom 4): f⁺ = −0.08826, f⁻ = 0.21006, f⁰ = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29832

Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): C (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.70839 — consistent with nucleophilic addition at the carbonyl carbon. Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): O (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742 — reflecting lone-pair density on the carbonyl oxygen. The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ (C atom), 0.70839.
